skill.name: data-scientist
skill.description: Guides advanced analytics, statistical modeling, machine learning workflows, and data storytelling for business decisions.
skill.summary: Apply data science methods to analyze data, build models, validate results, and communicate insights.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution alert is a false positive caused by Markdown inline code around a resource path. The weak cryptography alerts are also false positives from data science terminology, with no cryptographic implementation, shell execution, network access, credential access, or prompt injection found in the scanned SKILL.md file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 26 as Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the line uses Markdown inline code around a resource path. It does not execute a command or instruct shell use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 26 is a Markdown instruction to open a named resource file, not Ruby or shell syntax. No executable code or command arguments appear in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Analytics Terms Treated as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts occur on ordinary data science terms, including statistical methods, randomized trials, warehouse design, and example analytics prompts. No cryptographic implementation appears in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive Markdown content for data science capabilities and examples. I found no hash function, cipher call, digest API, or cryptographic code in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable analysis that connects statistics, machine learning, and business goals. This skill guides data exploration, model selection, validation, visualization, and stakeholder-ready recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Plans exploratory data analysis with summaries, distributions, outlier checks, and correlation review.
Recommends statistical tests, experiment designs, and causal inference approaches for business questions.
Guides supervised, unsupervised, time series, and anomaly detection modeling workflows.
Explains model evaluation, cross-validation, interpretability, and monitoring practices.
Helps translate analysis results into visualizations, reports, and decision recommendations.
limitations: It provides guidance and analysis structure, not automatic access to private datasets.
Results depend on data quality, sampling design, and clearly defined business objectives.
It does not replace legal, compliance, or human ethics review for regulated models.
Production deployment still requires engineering review, security controls, and operational monitoring.

use_cases.title: Customer Churn Modeling
use_cases.description: Analyze retention data, identify churn drivers, select modeling methods, and present actions for customer success teams.

use_cases.title: Experiment Design and Readout
use_cases.description: Design an A/B test, estimate sample size, choose metrics, evaluate results, and explain practical significance.

use_cases.title: Forecasting Operations Demand
use_cases.description: Plan a time series workflow for demand forecasting, seasonality checks, model comparison, and inventory decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for structuring a new data project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan an exploratory analysis for this dataset and business question. List the checks, metrics, charts, and assumptions I should review first.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Statistical Method
prompt_templates.prompt: Given my outcome, input variables, sample size, and decision goal, recommend suitable statistical methods. Explain assumptions, risks, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Modeling Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a predictive modeling workflow for my problem. Include feature engineering, model candidates, evaluation metrics, cross-validation, interpretability, and monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Analytical Decision
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert request for reviewing a high-impact analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed analysis plan for bias, leakage, confounding, statistical validity, business usefulness, and production readiness. Recommend specific corrections.

output_examples.input: A product team wants to measure whether a new checkout flow improves conversion.
output_examples.output: The skill would define primary and guardrail metrics, recommend randomization and sample size planning, outline significance testing, and explain how to interpret business impact.

output_examples.input: A finance team needs a fraud detection approach for transaction anomalies.
output_examples.output: The skill would compare anomaly detection and supervised modeling options, identify needed labels, suggest validation metrics, and highlight monitoring for drift and false positives.

output_examples.input: A marketing team wants customer segments for campaign planning.
output_examples.output: The skill would propose feature preparation, clustering methods, segment validation, visualization choices, and a plan to translate segments into campaign actions.

best_practices: Start with the business decision, success metric, and data-generating process before choosing methods.
Check leakage, missingness, sampling bias, and assumptions before trusting model results.
Report uncertainty, limitations, and practical significance alongside technical metrics.
anti_patterns: Choosing complex models before establishing a simple baseline and clear evaluation metric.
Treating correlation as causation without experimental design or causal reasoning.
Publishing dashboards or model scores without explaining data quality and confidence limits.

faq.question: What types of data science work does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports exploratory analysis, statistical testing, machine learning workflows, forecasting, segmentation, anomaly detection, and data storytelling.

faq.question: Can it build production machine learning systems by itself?
faq.answer: No. It can guide modeling and deployment planning, but production systems need engineering, security, and monitoring work.

faq.question: Does it require a specific programming language?
faq.answer: No. The skill references Python, R, SQL, and common analytics platforms, but its guidance can be adapted.

faq.question: How should I prepare a prompt for this skill?
faq.answer: Provide the business question, available data, target outcome, constraints, audience, and any current analysis results.

faq.question: Can it help with A/B testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide experiment design, metric choice, power analysis, statistical testing, and interpretation.

faq.question: How does it handle model risk and bias?
faq.answer: It encourages assumption checks, validation, interpretability, fairness review, monitoring, and clear documentation of limitations.
